g.text: monthly_story_quota : INT NULL [ADR-015 cost gate; NULL = platform default]
deactivated_at : TIMESTAMPTZ NULL [soft-deactivate; cascades to members]
is_admin : BOOLEAN [default false; capability orthogonal to role]
email : VARCHAR(320) NULL [contact only, never an identity key]
banned_themes : JSONB NULL [G2 guardian theme exclusions]
pin_hash : TEXT NULL [write-only PIN; never serialized]
monthly_request_envelope : INT NULL [ADR-015 G3 auto-approve cap]
CHECK (viewer consent id/at both NULL or both set)
CHECK (sharer consent id/at both NULL or both set)
version : INT [pinned to storybook_version via composite FK]
assigned_by : UUID «FK user.id, NULL» [NULL = system backfill]
approved_at : TIMESTAMPTZ NULL [ADR-015; set once on approve]
concept_id : UUID «FK concept.id, NULL» [set on approval]
actor_id : UUID «FK user.id, NULL» [NULL iff actor_role='system']
revoked_at : TIMESTAMPTZ NULL [set, not deleted, on revoke]
storybook_id : VARCHAR(120) NULL [NOT FK, see note]
node_id : VARCHAR(120) NULL [story-graph node id, never prose (ADR-016)]
CHECK (resolved_by/resolved_at both NULL or both set)
storybook_id is NOT a foreign key.
A job may fail before any storybook
row exists; a hard FK would block
Prevents saving state for a version
FK to user.id, annotated inline on the
owning column but not drawn as an arrow
never stored; only its jti and mint metadata
are. jti is UNIQUE so the revocation lookup
decision D1): written from the same
records; a DB trigger blocks UPDATE/
identify the row the event describes
entity_type varies per row, so no
